what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 inline SVG line icon depicting a stack of files, with a front document shape, folded corner detail, and an offset rear document outline.
observations: Uses an SVG viewBox of 0 0 24 24 with explicit width and height of 24.
Sets fill to none and stroke to currentColor for themeable monochrome rendering.
Uses stroke-width 1.5 with round line caps and round line joins.
Contains three path elements: a front page outline, a folded-corner line, and a rear offset page outline.
standouts: Folded-corner document motif paired with an offset stack line.
conventions: Uses inline SVG presentation attributes rather than CSS classes.
Keeps geometry path-based with no external references or embedded metadata.
concerns: No title, desc, role, or aria attributes are present for standalone accessibility semantics.
notes: A small reusable SVG icon for representing stacked files or multiple documents.
questions: Is this SVG intended to be decorative, or should accessible labeling be supplied by the consuming component?
Should the rear file remain an open partial outline, as drawn here?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename suggests a stacked files icon, and the SVG content draws overlapping document/file outlines.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a simple design-system icon for multiple files or a file stack, using stroke-only geometry that inherits surrounding text color.
novelty: The icon combines a folded-corner front document with a sparse rear-file outline in three minimal paths.
contribution: Contributes a compact, themeable stacked-files icon asset with simple stroke geometry suitable for reuse in interfaces that need a multiple-document or file-stack visual.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24px stroke-only SVG icon showing stacked files with a folded-corner front document and offset rear outline.
intention_for_embedding: It was made as a reusable UI icon for representing multiple files, documents, or a file stack while inheriting current text color.
